title: I Am, You Are, We All Learn
description: Bia confuses the verb TO BE. Dona Lúcia, Rafa, and Mr. Kumar help her untangle I am / you are / he is — in rhyme.
summary_pt_br: A Bia se confunde com o verbo TO BE. Dona Lúcia, Rafa e Mr. Kumar ajudam — em rimas e diálogos — a separar I am / you are / he is. Inclui a armadilha 'I have 16 years' (errada em inglês).

scenes.chapter: Chapter 4 — To Be or Not To Be
scenes.subtitle_en: The most important verb in English
scenes.subtitle_pt: O verbo mais importante do inglês

scenes.text_en:
Bia said, 'I HAVE sixteen years today.'
Mr. Kumar smiled gently, 'That's not how we say.
In English, for age, we use AM — so begin:
I AM sixteen years old. Try it again.'
scenes.text_pt:
Bia disse: 'EU TENHO dezesseis anos agora.'
Mr. Kumar sorriu: 'Assim não se fala, Bia menina.
No inglês, para idade, usamos AM — vem daqui:
I AM sixteen years old. Tenta de novo, aí!'

items.pt: é / está / são / estão (com you, we, they)

scenes.text_en:
I am a surfer. You are a friend.
He is my dad. She is his blend.
It is a dog — that's Cosmo the Lab.
We are a class. They are the gang.
Remember this rhythm. Don't let it slide.
Am, is, are — they're always inside.
scenes.text_pt:
I am surfista. You are amigo meu.
He is meu pai. She is o seu elo.
It is o cachorro — Cosmo, esse!
We are a turma. They are a galera.
Repita o ritmo. Não esqueça.
Am, is, are — estão sempre à espreita.

exercise.question: Complete: 'I ___ from Praia do Sol.' (Bia's line)
exercise.explanation: The pronoun I always takes AM. Contraction: I'm.

turns.pt: O Cosmo está SEMPRE com fome.

scenes.title: BE does the job of 'ser', 'estar', AND 'ter' (sometimes)
scenes.body_en: Portuguese has ser (permanent), estar (temporary), and sometimes uses ter for age/hunger. English uses ONE verb — BE — for all of it: 'I am a teacher' (ser), 'I am tired' (estar), 'I am 30 years old' (idade, não ter!), 'I am hungry' (fome, não ter fome!).
scenes.body_pt: Português separa ser/estar e às vezes usa 'ter' para idade/fome. O inglês usa só BE para tudo. 'I am' cobre 'sou', 'estou', 'tenho (idade)', 'estou com (fome/sede/medo)'.

exercise.question: Which one is correct English?
exercise.explanation: Age uses BE + number + 'years old'. 'Years old' is not optional — 'I am 30' is OK casually, but in a textbook, use 'years old'.
exercise.hint_pt_br: A fórmula certa: I am + número + years old. Nunca 'I have X years'.

exercise.explanation: Only I uses AM. He/she/it always use IS. Everyone else uses ARE.
exercise.hint_pt_br: Regra das três caixas: I=am, he/she/it=is, resto=are.

scenes.text_en:
Dona Lúcia, sixty-eight and kind,
read a letter she'd kept signed.
'My love, you ARE the sun,' it said.
'Sixty years,' she whispered, 'and I am not dead.'
Love, like BE, is simple and small —
just three forms, and they cover it all.
scenes.text_pt:
Dona Lúcia, sessenta e oito, gentil,
leu uma carta que guardou, num canto sutil.
'Meu amor, você É o sol,' dizia a página assim.
'Sessenta anos,' sussurrou, 'e ainda estou aqui.'
O amor, como o BE, é simples e leve —
três formas apenas, e tudo ele descreve.

exercise.question: Complete: 'My love, you ___ the sun.' (Dona Lúcia's letter)
exercise.explanation: YOU always takes ARE — whether talking to one person or many.
exercise.hint_pt_br: 'You' sempre pede 'are', não importa se é singular ou plural.

scenes.title: Recap reading
scenes.passage_en: At Amazing School, the class just finished "I Am, You Are, We All Learn". Bia confuses the verb TO BE. Dona Lúcia, Rafa, and Mr. Kumar help her untangle I am / you are / he is — in rhyme. Mr. Kumar reminded the students that practice is the key — listen, read aloud, write your own examples. Every small step adds up. The ocean in Praia do Sol keeps moving, and so does your English.

check.question: According to Mr. Kumar, what is the key to learning?
check.explanation: Practice — listen, read aloud, write your own examples.
check.hint_pt_br: A chave é a prática: ouvir, ler em voz alta, escrever seus próprios exemplos.

scenes.title: Listen to Bia
scenes.audio_text_en: Hello! I'm Bia, from Praia do Sol. Today in class we studied "I Am, You Are, We All Learn". I liked it because it was clear and short. Tomorrow we will practice again. See you then!

check.question: What did Bia study today?
options: A grammar rule about the past
I Am, You Are, We All Learn
check.explanation: She says: "Today in class we studied 'I Am, You Are, We All Learn'".
check.hint_pt_br: Ela fala claramente o título: "I Am, You Are, We All Learn".

scenes.title: Go deeper — free resources
scenes.body_pt: Fontes gratuitas e confiáveis para você estudar mais. Salve os links e volte sempre.
